business_name: Shawn Chaudhry - Yogurtland  - STATER BROS.
comments: When Indoor Media first approached me about putting a coupon on grocery store receipt tape, I honestly wasn’t interested. I thought it sounded outdated. I didn’t personally use receipt coupons, and no
searchable:
shawn chaudhry - yogurtland  - stater bros. when indoor media first approached me about putting a coupon on grocery store receipt tape, i honestly wasn’t interested. i thought it sounded outdated. i didn’t personally use receipt coupons, and none of my friends or family did either, so i assumed digital advertising would be the better route.

instead of doing the receipt coupons, i decided to run their digital ads. for about three months, we were getting visibility, but the problem was that i couldn’t actually track what it was doing for our sales. i’m a numbers person, so if i can’t clearly see the return, i basically assume it’s zero.

after sharing my frustration with the rep, he reminded me why the receipt coupons are different. they’re physical, they’re trackable, and they’re literally placed into the hands of local grocery shoppers right as they’re leaving the store.

at that point, i had already paid for the program, so we decided to run a "buy one get one" offer on the stater bros. receipt tape starting october 30.

the first month started a little slowly in november, but the second month started picking up, and by month three, the program was really working.

in february alone, we received 170 coupons.

what surprised me most was the type of customers coming in. it wasn’t just people grabbing one yogurt. families were coming in with the coupons and buying three, four, sometimes five bowls at a time. even with the bogo promotion, our average ticket was still around $22.
here’s how the numbers worked out for us:



average bowl price: $11

average ticket after bogo: $22

food cost: about 30%

cost of the promotion: about $3.30 per redemption



after food cost and the promotion cost, we were clearing about $12.10 per transaction.



with 170 redemptions in a month, that came out to about $2,057 in profit from the coupon customers alone.



our investment in the program was $450 per month.



once we saw those numbers, the decision was easy. we renewed the stater bros program early for our garden grove yogurtland, added an additional program in ralphs for the same location, and we also decided to run receipt tape advertising for our other business, yogurt passion in westminster.

i’ve also referred three friends who own ice cream shops to work with indoor media after seeing the results ourselves.

for us, the biggest difference was simple. digital ads gave us visibility, but the receipt coupons actually brought customers through the door — and we could track every single one.

 frozen yogurt, ice cream, toppings, dessert, snack, treat, ice cream cake, italian ice, custard, shakes, malts snow cones, froyo, gelato yogurtland ice cream / yogurt shops

business_name: Henry Perez  - Taco Life  - ALBERTSONS
comments: I’m a happy customer! We started this program at the beginning of October and went from 1 coupon a week to now seeing at least 3 coupons every single day. Our rep Caesar taught us exactly how to track
searchable: henry perez  - taco life  - albertsons i’m a happy customer! we started this program at the beginning of october and went from 1 coupon a week to now seeing at least 3 coupons every single day. our rep caesar taught us exactly how to track the program, so we see how much our average ticket is, how many new customers come in because of the coupon, and which offer they are using most. we staple every single coupon that comes in to the customer's receipt when they order. then we ask if they are visiting for their first time or if they have been here before. then we circle which offer they used. what we’ve noticed is our average ticket is $24, and the offers used are about a 50/50 split between the $5 off & the free taco. but the best part is that about 35% of the time coupons come in, they are new customers visiting us for their very first time. this program has outperformed any expectations we had, especially because i’m the guy who normally just throws the receipt away. we see single people and families come in; it’s a mixed bag. just trust the process, because when we first started and only saw 3 coupons come in our first month, we didn’t panic because caesar assured us from the start that it was completely normal, and that's why they do annual programs. i admit this advertising might seem old school, but i promise you it works!  mexican breakfast, lunch, dinner, taco, burrito, enchilada, quesadilla, tamales, margaritas, fajitas, chips, salsa, pozole, menudo, chile relleno, tortas, guacamole, tres leches, flan, churros, empanadas  taco life mexican

business_name: Alberto D'Calle - Gondola Pizza  - ALBERTSONS, STATER BROS.
comments: We started the register tape advertising in October of 2024. Honestly, we were hesitant to sign up because we figured nobody really looked at the back of receipts. We’ve been here for over 40 years an
searchable: alberto d'calle - gondola pizza  - albertsons, stater bros. we started the register tape advertising in october of 2024. honestly, we were hesitant to sign up because we figured nobody really looked at the back of receipts. we’ve been here for over 40 years and have never considered doing the program. the only reason we finally did it was because so many of the nearby businesses were getting great results. we thought, what the heck, it’s a tax write-off anyway, and what’s the worst that could happen? at first, it was slow. we didn’t get our first coupon until about 3 weeks in. but by month 3, we were averaging 5 coupons a week. by month 6, it was 10. by month 9, it was 20. and by month 11, we were getting more than 30 coupons a week. even better, about one in five of those customers were visiting us for the very first time. for a restaurant that’s been around for 40 years, that’s huge because your regulars don’t last forever. you always need new faces coming through the door. our quality speaks for itself, so the biggest win for us was simply getting new people to try us. we’re averaging more than twenty new customers a month because of this program. what surprised me most was that in the beginning, it was mostly older people using the coupons, but around month eight or nine, we started seeing a lot of younger customers in their 30s coming in. i guess the saying is true, with this generation, you have to tell them 100 times before they finally listen. if you want my honest opinion, i don’t have money to waste, and we just renewed for another year. that should tell you everything you need to know. thank you, indoor media, for sending chu out and convincing me to sign up. i look forward to many more years working with you guys pizza restaurant dinner, delivery, wings, calzone, breadsticks, pasta, carry out, pepperoni, sausage, thin crust, subs, calzones, stromboli, salad, pizza buffet gondola pizza pizza
